message:
chore: close bd-iuvb.17.10 (Visuals screens epic complete)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -464,7 +464,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2k018.22","title":"Preset preview gallery screen (demo showcase)","description":"Purpose:
- Provide a dedicated demo screen that previews all TableTheme presets side‑by‑side so users can quickly evaluate aesthetics and legibility.

Background / rationale:
- Users requested more presets and explicit guidance; a gallery makes selection obvious and accelerates adoption.
- A gallery also acts as a regression surface for theme changes across terminal sizes.

Scope:
- Add a showcase screen that renders the same dataset across all presets.
- Include both widget Table and markdown Table representations if feasible, to confirm visual parity.
- Display preset name + a short descriptor (e.g., “solar: warm/high‑contrast”).
- For terminal_classic, show ANSI baseline with modern‑terminal enhancement (if available) to ensure it doesn’t look worse on modern terminals.

Test surfaces:
- Add snapshots for the gallery at 80x24 and 120x40.
- Ensure gallery is included in E2E logs (preset_id, phase, style hash, column widths).

Constraints:
- Deterministic phase for any animation in gallery.
- Avoid heavy per‑cell computations; reuse existing render paths.

Acceptance:
- Gallery screen exists and is discoverable from demo navigation.
- All presets render legibly at both sizes.
- Widget/markdown parity is visually consistent.
- Snapshot + E2E logs capture the gallery.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:20:06.276090787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:26.263200075Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:26.263179607Z","close_reason":"Gallery screen, snapshots, and E2E JSONL logging added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.22","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:20:06.276090787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.22","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:20:10.885017674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.22","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:20:13.953766782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.22","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:20:16.464162006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k018.23","title":"Interactive preset gallery controls (keyboard + live overrides)","description":"Purpose:
- Make the preset preview gallery highly interactive so users can explore presets and live overrides without leaving the screen.

Scope:
- Keyboard controls to cycle presets and toggle between widget/markdown view.
- Live override toggles (e.g., header emphasis, zebra strength, border style, highlight row).
- On‑screen help legend for controls.
- Persist selection per session so exploration feels continuous.

Testing:
- Snapshot tests for default state (controls visible, deterministic).
- E2E logs include selected preset, toggles state, and current view mode.

Acceptance:
- Gallery is fully navigable by keyboard.
- Live overrides update immediately and deterministically.
- E2E logs capture interactive state for debugging.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.24","title":"Interactive gallery input-state tests (keyboard + toggles)","description":"Purpose:
- Validate the interactive preset gallery state machine (keyboard navigation + live overrides) without relying solely on E2E runs.

Scope:
- Unit tests for key bindings: next/prev preset, toggle widget/markdown view, toggle header emphasis/zebra/border/highlight.
- Verify deterministic state transitions (no hidden time dependence).
- Ensure persisted session state is restored correctly when re-entering the screen.

Logging:
- On failure, log initial state, input sequence, and resulting state.

Acceptance:
- Input handling is deterministic and fully covered by unit tests.
- Tests fail with actionable logs when mappings regress.

-{"id":"bd-2k018.25","title":"Custom preset save + export/import (JSON)","description":"Purpose:
- Allow users to save a customized TableTheme from the interactive gallery and export/import it as JSON.

Scope:
- Provide an in‑app action to save the current overrides as a named preset.
- Export preset to JSON (schema documented) and import from JSON into runtime presets.
- Persist custom presets per session (and optionally to disk if supported by demo harness).
- Support export to file and clipboard copy when available in the demo environment.

Testing:
- Unit tests for JSON schema round‑trip and deterministic serialization.
- E2E logs include exported preset name + checksum when export/import is exercised.

Acceptance:
- Users can save a preset, export it (file or clipboard), and re‑import to restore identical styling.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.26","title":"Custom preset schema + validation tests","description":"Purpose:
- Ensure exported/imported TableTheme presets are validated and stable across versions.

Scope:
- Define a strict JSON schema (fields, enums, ranges) with explicit size limits for safety.
- Add validation errors for unknown fields or invalid ranges.
- Provide forward‑compatibility notes (allow optional fields with defaults).

Testing:
- Unit tests for valid/invalid fixtures; log validation error details.
- Snapshot test for canonical JSON output ordering.
- Tests for size‑limit enforcement and unknown‑field handling.

Acceptance:
- Invalid presets fail with actionable error logs.
- Valid presets round‑trip without loss.
","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: Added TableThemeSpec validation (range/size limits + effect/gradient checks), TableThemeSpecError, and deny_unknown_fields on spec structs; added unit tests for validation. Supports bd-2k018.25/26.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"OrangePond","created_at":"2026-02-05T04:57:33.967076831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:59:40.580438943Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:59:40.580420598Z","close_reason":"Completed: documented TableThemeSpec JSON export/import schema + example in docs/specs/table-theme.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.27","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T04:57:33.967076831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k018.3","title":"Implement table animation resolver (phase-driven styles)","description":"Purpose:
- Implement deterministic, phase-driven style transforms for table animation.

Implementation detail:
- Provide a resolver that lerps fg/bg colors based on a phase input (0..1).
- Use low-amplitude curves (breathing / pulse) to keep effects tasteful.
- Effects applied at row/column/section level only (avoid per-cell heavy ops).

Phase handling:
- Normalize phase via clamp/wrap semantics from spec (e.g., fract for >1).
- Apply per-effect phase_offset deterministically.

Suggested formula:
- breathing(t) = sin curve with asymmetry (quick inhale, slow exhale).
- alpha = min + (max-min) * breathing(t).
- style = lerp(base, highlight, alpha).

Performance:
- O(1) per row/column resolution; avoid allocations.
- No global time; caller passes phase (deterministic, testable).

Acceptance:
- Resolver produces stable output for identical phase inputs.
- Effect intensity is subtle by default (no visual noise).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:37.667080959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:59:13.977273020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:59:13.977254816Z","close_reason":"Implemented phase-driven table effect resolver","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:37.667080959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:53:45.239956359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:41:17.825162423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1149,15 +1149,15 @@
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.16","title":"Demo: Navigation IA (Screen Registry + Palette + Category Tabs)","description":"Background
- With 30+ screens, navigation can become overwhelming without a strong IA.
- A dedicated registry and palette make the demo feel intentional and fast.

Goals
- Implement Screen Registry (id/title/short_label/category/order/palette_tags/blurb/hotkey).
- Implement category tabs + category-aware navigation.
- Implement Screen Palette (Ctrl+K) with search, filters, favorites, and ranking.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Tour/Core/Visuals/Interaction/Text/Systems displayed with color pills.
- Tab bar shows short_label + category color.
- Shift+Left/Right jumps categories; Left/Right moves within category.

Implementation Notes
- Registry is the single source of truth for screen list and ordering.
- Palette ranking: exact > prefix > word-start > substring (stable tie-breakers).
- Favorites are persisted in memory for session; optional future persistence.

Acceptance Criteria
- Registry drives tab bar, palette, and help overlay (no duplicated lists).
- Palette search is fast and deterministic (fixed ordering for equal scores).
- Category navigation works from any screen.
- All screens are registered with category, label, tags, and blurb.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for registry ordering and palette ranking.
- Snapshot tests for palette (empty, filtered, favorites) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, action, query, selected_screen, category, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacOwl","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103372323Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103354269Z","close_reason":"Completed: palette logging, snapshots, ranking test, help legend, E2E step","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.16","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17","title":"Demo UX: Mouse-first interactivity + shortcut expansion","description":"Background
- Demo showcase already has a strong screen catalog, Screen Registry, command palette, and a Guided Tour.
- Mouse support exists but is currently shallow: tab bar click-switching is wired via the hit grid, and a handful of screens register hit regions, but most screens do not respond to mouse in an intuitive 'click what you see' way.
- Many screens have keyboard shortcuts, but lots of interaction is either hidden or passive; the demo should feel alive and exploratory.

Goals
- Make every screen in `ftui-demo-showcase` meaningfully mouse-friendly:
  - Clickable controls/widgets respond as users expect.
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the thing under the pointer (lists, panels, editors).
  - Drag interactions work where appropriate (kanban, drag-drop, timeline scrubbing, sliders).
  - Hover affordances are visible (focus/active state, subtle highlight, status hints).
- Add more keyboard shortcuts that *do things* (not just navigation):
  - Consistent global shortcuts (help, palette, perf HUD, inspector, toggle mouse).
  - Per-screen shortcuts for toggling modes, adjusting knobs, and triggering demos.
- Upgrade the Guided Tour storyboard to include the 'best stuff' and explicitly demonstrate:
  - Inline mode value (scrollback preserved; chrome stable).
  - Determinism + checksums + evidence ledgers.
  - Hit testing + hyperlinks + inspector overlays.
  - Performance HUD + budgets + degradation behavior.

Hard Constraints / Invariants (non-negotiable)
- Preserve Inline Mode scrollback as a first-class property:
  - Default behavior must not silently steal terminal scrollback unless user opts in.
  - Mouse capture policy must be explicit and documented in the demo UI and CLI.
- Keep one-writer rule / terminal lifecycle correctness intact.
- Determinism: fixed seeds + time-freeze remain deterministic across mouse/keyboard interactions.
- Performance: hit testing and hover updates must not materially regress frame time; coalesce high-frequency mouse move events.

Scope Notes
- This epic is about demo UX and interaction plumbing. It may require small changes in `ftui-core`/`ftui-widgets` (e.g., semantic mouse events or richer hit regions) when needed, but avoid large API churn.
- Prefer building shared primitives (clickable list/table/scrollbar/etc.) once, then applying to screens.

Success Criteria
- Every screen under `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/` has:
  - At least one meaningful mouse interaction (click/scroll/drag/hover) that changes state.
  - At least 3-5 screen-specific shortcuts that are discoverable in the help overlay.
- Guided Tour v2 covers the highest-signal screens and interactions.
- Test coverage exists:
  - Unit tests for mouse dispatch + focus/hover rules.
  - Deterministic integration tests per interaction category.
  - PTY/E2E validation where feasible.

Key References (starting points)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid enablement + tab click handler)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges for chrome/panes)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (mouse capture enable/disable)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (semantic click/drag abstractions)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` + `spatial_hit_index.rs` (hit grid implementation)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"PinkRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:09:20.407652344Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","mouse","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":817,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution Notes / Dependency Shape
- Foundation tasks:
  - bd-iuvb.17.1 (mouse policy / inline scrollback) blocks bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) and bd-iuvb.17.6 (tour v2) and bd-iuvb.17.7 (tests).
  - bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) blocks bd-iuvb.17.4 (chrome mouse).
  - bd-iuvb.17.3 (widget hit semantics) and bd-iuvb.17.5 (shortcut standards) are shared prerequisites for essentially all screen work.
- Screen tasks are grouped under sub-epics by ScreenCategory:
  - Tour: bd-iuvb.17.8
  - Core: bd-iuvb.17.9
  - Visuals: bd-iuvb.17.10
  - Interaction: bd-iuvb.17.11
  - Text: bd-iuvb.17.12
  - Systems: bd-iuvb.17.13
- Guided Tour v2 (bd-iuvb.17.6) is explicitly blocked on the highest-signal interactive screens (dashboard, inline mode, determinism, time travel, hyperlinks, perf HUD, etc.) so we don’t ship a tour that skips the good stuff.

Suggested Parallelization
- Do bd-iuvb.17.1 and bd-iuvb.17.2 early, because they define the click/scroll/drag contract.
- bd-iuvb.17.3 (widgets) can proceed in parallel and will unblock many screens quickly.
- Once the dispatcher exists, screens can be upgraded independently by category.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:58Z"},{"id":852,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Landed Dashboard mouse/hover fixes:
- Dashboard now tracks hover separately from focus and dims panel border on hover.
- Mouse dispatcher treats Dashboard pane tiles as pane-links: activate on MouseUp w/ drag-threshold, but don’t consume hover-move events.
- Updated unit test to find a Dashboard pane-hit cell via hit grid instead of hard-coded coords.

Commits: e26016c7 (also includes test robustness in app.rs + hover styling in dashboard.rs).","created_at":"2026-02-06T09:09:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","title":"Demo Input: mouse capture policy + inline scrollback preservation","description":"Background
- Mouse capture is currently available (see `ftui-core` terminal session enable/disable and the demo's `--no-mouse` CLI flag), but we must not compromise FrankenTUI's core value proposition: Inline Mode preserves native terminal scrollback.
- In many terminals, enabling mouse reporting steals the scroll wheel from the terminal's scrollback. That is acceptable in AltScreen mode, but it is a footgun in Inline mode unless it is explicit and opt-in.

Goal
- Define and implement a clear, explicit mouse capture policy for the demo showcase that:
  - Preserves native scrollback in Inline mode by default.
  - Still allows a 'full mouse' experience in AltScreen mode by default.
  - Provides an obvious, discoverable toggle and CLI/env config.

Design / Policy (proposed)
- Introduce a demo-level `MouseMode` concept (names are bikesheddable):
  - `Off`: no mouse capture; scroll wheel is terminal scrollback.
  - `On`: mouse capture enabled (click/scroll/drag/hover routed to UI).
- Defaults:
  - AltScreen: `On`.
  - Inline: `Off` (unless user explicitly opts in).
- UI affordances:
  - Status bar shows `Mouse: OFF (scrollback)` vs `Mouse: ON (UI scroll)`.
  - Help overlay documents toggle.
- Controls:
  - Keybinding: `M` toggles mouse mode.
  - CLI: keep `--no-mouse`; add `--mouse=on|off` (or env) if needed for determinism in tests.

Key Constraints
- No surprise scrollback theft in Inline mode.
- Must not violate one-writer rule or terminal teardown invariants.
- Deterministic: mode toggles are explicit inputs; no terminal-dependent heuristics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode demo retains native scrollback by default (wheel scrolls scrollback).
- AltScreen demo remains fully mouse interactive by default.
- Mouse toggle is visible/discoverable (status + help), and is logged in JSONL if demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for CLI/env parsing and default selection based on screen mode.
- PTY E2E smoke: Inline mode launch asserts mouse capture is disabled by default; AltScreen asserts enabled.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (Enable/Disable mouse capture)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` (mouse flags)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (event routing)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:20:08.430042660Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:16:01.310846497Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","input","mouse","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":815,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyGorge claiming this bead. Will implement mouse capture policy with MouseMode enum, screen-mode-aware defaults, M keybinding toggle, status bar indicator, and CLI flags.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:39Z"},{"id":833,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"EmeraldAnchor","text":"Note: ftui-runtime still has a stale TODO/no-op for mouse capture. In crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs:2191-2195, Cmd::SetMouseCapture(enabled) currently ignores enabled; TerminalSession already has set_mouse_capture(bool) in crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs:393. Wiring execute_cmd to call self.session.set_mouse_capture(enabled)? should unblock demo mouse-mode work.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:14:38Z"},{"id":837,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon: Completed demo-showcase integration for mouse capture policy. Changes: CLI --mouse=on|off|auto flag, resolve_mouse_enabled() in main.rs, ToggleMouseCapture msg/handler with Cmd::SetMouseCapture, F6/m keybindings, status bar Mouse:ON/OFF indicator, help overlay entry, command palette action, Down+Up mouse click infrastructure, all 1427 lib tests + 244 snapshot tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:20:08Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Visuals screens","description":"Purpose
- Track mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for Visuals-category screens.

Included Screens
- Data Viz
- Table Theme Gallery
- Mermaid Showcase
- Mermaid Mega Showcase
- Visual Effects
- Theme Studio
- Snapshot Player (Time Travel Studio)

Visuals Interaction Themes
- Mouse for pan/zoom/inspect where meaningful (diagrams, charts).
- Wheel adjusts scale or scrolls within the appropriate panel, never 'randomly'.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","title":"Data Viz: click-to-toggle series + mouse inspect + shortcuts","description":"Background
- DataViz is a dense visuals screen; it should support mouse inspection and fast toggles.

Goal
- Make charts feel interactive: click legends, hover points, wheel scroll/zoom where appropriate.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover a chart point shows tooltip (in status hint or overlay).
- Click legend toggles series visibility.
- Wheel adjusts zoom scale or scrolls within the selected chart panel (consistent rule).

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `L`: toggle legend.
- `Z`: toggle zoom mode.
- `R`: reset view.

Acceptance Criteria
- Interactions are deterministic (same inputs -> same tooltip/selection).
- At least 3-5 shortcuts are available and discoverable.

Key References

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.2","title":"Table Theme Gallery: click-to-select preset + scroll + compare shortcuts","description":"Background
- TableThemeGallery is a perfect 'click around and compare' screen.

Goal
- Make theme selection and comparison mouse-first, and add shortcuts for rapid cycling.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover highlights a preset tile.
- Click selects preset.
- Wheel scrolls preset list and/or adjusts animation phase if present.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `J/K`: next/prev preset.
- `Space`: pause/resume theme animation (if applicable).
- `C`: toggle comparison layout.

Acceptance Criteria
- Preset selection is obvious with hover/click.
- Snapshot includes selected preset state.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","title":"Mermaid Showcase: mouse interactions for controls + diagram inspection","description":"Background
- MermaidShowcase already has rich keyboard controls, but mouse support should match expectations: click tabs, click samples, scroll panels.

Goal
- Add mouse-driven selection and inspection across the Mermaid showcase UI.

Mouse Interactions
- Click sample list entries.
- Wheel scrolls sample list and/or output pane depending on hover.
- If diagram supports hit regions: hover highlights node/edge; click selects.

Keyboard Shortcuts
- Ensure existing shortcuts are surfaced, and add a small number of high-signal ones (reset, toggle overlays, toggle glyph mode).

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse-only exploration is viable for sample switching and scrolling.
- No regression in determinism/snapshots.

Coordination
- This task should coordinate with the Mermaid showcase epics (`bd-2nkmi`, `bd-309ob`) but focuses on UX wiring, not layout algorithms.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/mermaid_showcase.rs`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:15:29.079143741Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T19:15:29.079121229Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":897,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WindyOwl claiming.  {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","title":"Mermaid Mega Showcase: mouse-first control surface + diagram hit testing","description":"Background
- MermaidMegaShowcase is the over-the-top interactive lab screen. It should be *extremely* mouse-friendly.

Goal
- Ensure the mega screen's control surface (knobs, toggles, sample selection, overlays) is fully operable by mouse and has rich shortcuts.

Mouse Interactions
- Click any toggle/knob.
- Drag sliders where present.
- Wheel scrolls the control panel or diagram pane depending on hover.
- Diagram hit testing: hover highlights; click selects; drag pans (if supported).

Keyboard Shortcuts
- Add consistent keys for:
  - sample next/prev
  - toggle overlays
  - start/stop auto-run
  - reset state deterministically

Acceptance Criteria
- Mega screen feels like a control panel: nothing important is keyboard-only.
- Interactions do not regress performance; mouse move is coalesced.

Coordination
- Coordinate with the mega screen epic `bd-3oaig` (this task focuses on mouse/shortcut UX wiring).

Key References

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","title":"Visual Effects: mouse controls for anchors/params + shortcut presets","description":"Background
- VisualEffects is a 'wow' screen. Mouse should control something immediate (anchor, look, parameter).

Goal
- Add obvious mouse controls (move/drag) and shortcuts for effect presets.

Mouse Interactions
- Mouse move updates an anchor or inspection point (already used in some harnesses).
- Drag adjusts one key parameter (e.g., intensity) with deterministic step.
- Wheel cycles effects or adjusts a focused parameter.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `N/P`: next/prev effect.
- `Space`: pause/resume animation.
- `R`: reset deterministic seed.

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse interaction produces visible effect and is deterministic under fixed seed/time.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentFinch","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:38.406121353Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:38.105733279Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","vfx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":873,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SilentFinch verified: mouse handling, keybindings, hit-testing implemented. 160+ lib tests pass. - Make palette editing mouse-first (click swatches, drag sliders) with shortcuts for rapid toggles.

Mouse Interactions
- Click swatch selects color.
- Drag sliders adjusts channels deterministically.
- Wheel nudges selected channel or scrolls panel.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Tab`: next field.
- `C`: copy/paste color (demo-local).
- `R`: reset to preset.

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse-only palette edits are viable.
- Help overlay documents actions.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.7","title":"Snapshot Player: mouse scrubbing + click-to-jump + shortcuts","description":"Background
- SnapshotPlayer (Time Travel Studio) is one of the 'best stuff' screens.

Goal
- Make time travel controls mouse-first: click to jump, drag to scrub, wheel to step.

Mouse Interactions
- Click timeline to jump to frame.
- Drag timeline thumb to scrub.
- Wheel steps frame-by-frame when hovered.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Space`: play/pause.
- `←/→`: prev/next frame.
- `A/B`: toggle comparison modes.
- `R`: reset session.

Acceptance Criteria
- Scrubbing is deterministic and does not flicker.
- Snapshot tests cover a non-zero frame selection state.

Key References

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.1","title":"Forms & Input: click-to-focus fields + mouse wheel + shortcuts","description":"Background
- Forms are one of the most common interaction patterns. This screen should feel like a real UI.

Goal
- Make all controls operable with mouse and provide shortcuts for power navigation.

Mouse Interactions
- Click to focus a field.
- Click toggles and buttons to activate.
- Wheel scrolls the form when content overflows.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Tab`/`Shift+Tab`: next/prev control.
- `Enter`: activate primary action.
- `Ctrl+R`: reset form state.

Acceptance Criteria
- Focus is consistent between mouse and keyboard.
- Help overlay lists interaction keys.

Key References

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.2","title":"File Browser: click tree/navigation + wheel scroll + open shortcuts","description":"Background
- FileBrowser is naturally mouse-driven: click to expand, click to open/preview.

Goal
- Make the file browser fully mouse-friendly and add shortcuts for fast navigation.

Mouse Interactions
- Click folder to expand/collapse.
- Click file to preview.
- Double click file to 'open' (demo-local action).
- Wheel scrolls tree or preview pane based on hover.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Enter`: open/preview selection.
- `Backspace`: go up.
- `/`: focus search.
- `R`: refresh (deterministic).

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse-only navigation is viable.
- Pane routing is deterministic and tested.

Key References

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.3","title":"Macro Recorder: click record/play + timeline scrubbing + shortcuts","description":"Background
- MacroRecorder is a great place to demonstrate deterministic replay of input.

Goal
- Make recording and replay controls mouse-clickable and add shortcuts to drive scenarios.

Mouse Interactions
- Click Record/Stop/Play.
- Click timeline to jump to an event.
- Wheel scrolls event list.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Space`: play/pause.
- `R`: start/stop record.
- `P`: play.

Acceptance Criteria
- Replay is deterministic; macro UI is fully operable via mouse.

Key References
